metric_value: RAS-augmentation: add enterprise-grade HA/clustering/TP-monitor on top of NT
notes: NCR differentiates from plain NT via LifeKeeper, TOP END, WorldMark, and NMC4000 management tools

metric_value: Automatic failover in 2 seconds to 5 minutes; up to 3-node NT cluster
notes: Users confirmed no-downtime application upgrades via LifeKeeper live migration

metric_value: Redirects client transactions around failed servers, apps, or network nodes
notes: Users described TOP END as 'client-server software-equivalent for fault-tolerant OLTP on NT'

metric_value: 20-25% reduction in OLTP application deployment time; some sites reported 50%
notes: RAD = Rapid Application Deployment; NCR users confirmed faster deployment using component-level testing with LifeKeeper harness

metric_name: SQL Server cost advantage vs alternatives
metric_value: Microsoft SQL Server 6.5 on NT up to 90% less costly than Unix/OpenVMS/MVS RDBMS alternatives
notes: Total cost comparison cited by IS decision-makers; vendor-reported figure

metric_value: NT/SQL Server to scale to 8 CPUs with NT Server 4.0 (fall 1996); 12-16 CPUs by mid-1997
notes: NCR and Microsoft joint roadmap prediction; based on NCR engineering collaboration with Microsoft

metric_value: Windows NT Server 4.0 Enterprise Edition (Sept 1997) supported 8-way SMP clustering; SQL Server 7.0 (1998) supported 8+ CPUs
notes: NT 4.0 EE released Sept 1997 with clustering; prediction partially accurate - 8 CPU target met, 16-CPU timeline slightly delayed

metric_value: NCR to introduce SMP Utilization Manager for NT in 1996 for 16-way WorldMark systems
notes: Planned product announcement; allows IS to assign apps to specific CPUs on large SMP

metric_value: NCR delivered SMP management tools; absorbed into later Teradata and NT product lines; specific 'Utilization Manager' branding not widely documented post-1997
notes: Product likely shipped but NCR's hardware server line transitioned as NT evolved; verification limited

metric_value: NCR to provide NT-based data mart solutions starting with NT Server 4.0; data-mart-up/down routing to Teradata

notes: NCR planned to leverage Teradata expertise for NT departmental data marts

metric_value: NCR/Teradata became leading enterprise data warehouse platform in late 1990s; NT-based data marts proliferated broadly as predicted
notes: Teradata remained NCR's flagship data warehouse product; NT data marts became mainstream by 1998-2000

metric_name: Aberdeen overall verdict on NCR NT
metric_value: NCR is an 'experienced, fail-safe choice for NT-based OLTP application deployments'
notes: Based on user interviews with major retail, finance, telecom, and transportation NCR/NT early adopters

metric_name: AT&T GIS enterprise NT commitment announcement
metric_value: August 1994: AT&T GIS announced plan to bring NT into enterprise with scalability, manageability, reliability, serviceability features
notes: AT&T GIS became NCR after AT&T spin-off announced Sept 1995, completed 1996

metric_name: NT OLTP benefit factor 1: Time to market
metric_value: IT decision makers primary motivation: NT enables faster competitive response than legacy mainframe
notes: Market window shrinking from years to months; MVS-based systems too slow to adapt

metric_name: NT OLTP benefit factor 2: Service level agreements
metric_value: NCR LifeKeeper/TOP END enabled IS to commit to enterprise-grade SLAs, including external customer-facing guarantees
source_page: NCR's Impact: Ensures New Service-Level Agreements
notes: Some enterprises offered contractual SLA guarantees to external customers enabled by NCR HA tools

metric_name: NT OLTP benefit factor 3: Homogeneous environment
metric_value: IS decision makers sought single standard OS platform across hardware; reduced management complexity
notes: Contrast with heterogeneous Unix/VMS/MVS environments requiring platform-specific expertise

metric_name: NT OLTP benefit factor 4: Desktop synergy
metric_value: Business-line managers wanted OLTP on NT to align with desktop Windows; reduced training, OLE object reuse
notes: NT Workstation 4.0 alignment with NT Server cited as strategic advantage

metric_name: NCR Microsoft Authorized Consulting Partner status

notes: Based on NCR's growing NT implementation expertise and existing Microsoft Authorized Support Center status
